framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Reach the top tier of competitive machine learning (Kaggle Grandmaster)
paths.what_it_is: Repeatedly place at the top of public, leaderboard-scored data science and ML competitions until the platform itself certifies you at its highest rank, producing a fully public, objective track record of modeling skill.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves modeling skill through a fully public, objective track record — a verifiable rank instead of a self-reported claim.

paths.path: Build and maintain an open-source data tool that the industry runs on
paths.what_it_is: Write and keep maintaining a foundational open-source library or engine for the data stack - a dataframe library, a parallel-compute framework, a database engine - visible in a public commit and release history, not a one-off internal script.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can build and sustain load-bearing infrastructure that other practitioners depend on daily, not just a one-off internal script.

paths.path: Publish technical writing that becomes the canonical explainer in the field
paths.what_it_is: Write and consistently ship public technical explanations - a blog, a newsletter, an illustrated deep-dive - that other practitioners cite and pass around as the clearest account of a hard data or ML concept, rather than writing only for an internal audience.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves you can explain a hard data or ML concept clearly enough that other practitioners cite and pass it around — sustained explanation, not one viral post.
